Clever betrayal that works once: He told the miller that a bottle of Evening Luck would triple any harvest if poured at dusk. The miller trusted him; the harvest bloomed beyond belief that year. Children danced, elders sang, and Nimbo pocketed the profits as the town glittered with abundance. But the soil remembered truth, and the bloom collapsed the next season; the lie devoured memory.

Detection: A girl with salt-stung cheeks and a storm-touched raven watched the grain sift into nothing. The raven spoke a warning in an old language the valley remembered; the girl traced the deceit in the ledger, saw the numbers refuse to breathe.

Punishment costly to carry out: The river-goddess demanded restitution; Nimbo must replace every ruined grain with his own hands for seven harvests, and renounce mischief while the fields regrew.

Reason the cycle ends: When honor returns and fear dissolves, the trickster’s voice is silenced by truth; no one trusts him again today. The valley learns to listen to reason.
